Here are a few examples of how to generate Terraform configurations:

Example output 1:

  byte_length = 4
}

  length  = 16
  special = false
}

  identifier          = "metricbeat-test-${random_id.suffix.hex}"

}

response:
```hcl
resource "random_id" "suffix" {
  byte_length = 4
}

resource "random_password" "db" {
  length  = 16
  special = false
}

# DB Subnet Group spanning the 3 Availability Zones
resource "aws_db_subnet_group" "example" {
  name       = "mysql-cluster-subnet-group"
  # Replace these with actual Subnet IDs in your VPC
  subnet_ids = ["subnet-1a", "subnet-1b", "subnet-1c"] 
}

# Security Group for the Cluster
resource "aws_security_group" "example" {
  name_prefix = "mysql-cluster-sg"
  # Replace with your actual VPC ID
  vpc_id      = "vpc-12345678"

  ingress {
    from_port   = 3306
    to_port     = 3306
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
}

resource "aws_rds_cluster" "example" {
  cluster_identifier      = "mysql-cluster-${random_id.suffix.hex}"
  engine                  = "aurora-mysql"
  engine_version          = "8.0.mysql_aurora.3.02.0"
  database_name           = "mydb"
  master_username         = "admin"
  master_password         = random_password.db.result
  db_subnet_group_name    = aws_db_subnet_group.example.name
  vpc_security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.example.id]
  availability_zones      = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
  skip_final_snapshot     = true
  # Note: Aurora automatically manages storage. It starts at a minimum (usually 10GB) 
  # and scales automatically, so 'allocated_storage' is not configurable.
}

resource "aws_rds_cluster_instance" "instances" {
  count              = 3
  identifier         = "mysql-cluster-${random_id.suffix.hex}-${count.index}"
  cluster_identifier = aws_rds_cluster.example.id
  instance_class     = "db.t3.small"
  engine             = aws_rds_cluster.example.engine
  availability_zone  = element(["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"], count.index)
}
```

# Custom Neptune Cluster Parameter Group
resource "aws_neptune_cluster_parameter_group" "custom_cluster_pg" {
  name        = "custom-neptune-cluster-pg"
  family      = "neptune1"
  description = "Custom Neptune cluster parameter group"

  parameter {
    name  = "neptune_query_timeout"
    value = "120000"
  }
}

# Custom Neptune Instance Parameter Group
resource "aws_neptune_parameter_group" "custom_instance_pg" {
  name        = "custom-neptune-instance-pg"
  family      = "neptune1"
  description = "Custom Neptune instance parameter group"

  parameter {
    name  = "neptune_query_timeout"
    value = "120000"
  }
}

# Subnet Group (Required for Neptune Cluster)
# Note: Replace subnet_ids with your actual Subnet IDs
resource "aws_neptune_subnet_group" "example" {
  name       = "neptune-subnet-group"
  subnet_ids = ["subnet-xxxxxxxx", "subnet-yyyyyyyy"]
}

# Security Group (Required for Neptune Cluster)
# Note: Replace vpc_id with your actual VPC ID
resource "aws_security_group" "example" {
  name        = "neptune-security-group"
  description = "Allow Neptune inbound traffic"
  vpc_id      = "vpc-xxxxxxxx"

  ingress {
    from_port   = 8182
    to_port     = 8182
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }
}

# Neptune Cluster
resource "aws_neptune_cluster" "example" {
  cluster_identifier              = "neptune-cluster-demo"
  engine                          = "neptune"
  engine_version                  = "1.2.1.0"
  backup_retention_period         = 5
  preferred_backup_window         = "07:00-09:00"
  skip_final_snapshot             = true
  apply_immediately               = true
  neptune_cluster_parameter_group_name = aws_neptune_cluster_parameter_group.custom_cluster_pg.name
  neptune_subnet_group_name       = aws_neptune_subnet_group.example.name
  vpc_security_group_ids          = [aws_security_group.example.id]
}

# Neptune Cluster Instance
resource "aws_neptune_cluster_instance" "example" {
  count              = 1
  identifier         = "neptune-instance-demo"
  cluster_identifier = aws_neptune_cluster.example.id
  engine             = "neptune"
  engine_version     = "1.2.1.0"
  instance_class     = "db.t4g.medium"
  apply_immediately  = true
  neptune_parameter_group_name = aws_neptune_parameter_group.custom_instance_pg.name
}
